mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with a p-value below 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates rising capacity utilization and manufacturing output, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates show zero predictive power for 20-day SPX direction during mid-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows since 2005.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising real retail sales validate robust aggregate demand, reinforcing earnings revision cycles and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in predicting 60-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production combined with contracting housing starts signals a synchronized supply-side contraction, prompting equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when both INDPRO and HOUST decline simultaneously during late-cycle phases.
